Weave-Bot — Precision Automation for Handloom Tie & Dye
DSF Contract 2026-2733 · Project IN-2025-3-03 · Status as of 23 Jun 2026
FinanceBudgetProcurement RoadmapImpactEngineering
Project Status Dashboard · Tranche 1

Weave‑Bot

A 10-foot automated coloring bed that digitises the 37-step resist-dye process of Odisha Ikat — preserving the poetry on the loom while lifting the physical burden on weavers. Funded by the Dassault Systèmes Foundation, built at Centurion University.

DSF grant₹23,45,000 CUTM contribution₹11,55,000 Tranche 1 released30 Apr 2026

Two funding streams run in parallel. The Foundation's first tranche covers equipment; the University's own contribution has stood up the dedicated project laboratory.

02 · Utilisation by component

Nine budget heads, dyed by spend

Each bar's length is the sanctioned amount; the dyed portion is what's committed against it — the way a resist-dyed thread takes colour only where it's meant to.

Sanctioned total ₹35,00,000 Committed ₹11,55,127 Bar scale ∝ sanctioned amount · dye ∝ committed
03 · Procurement ledger

Four tax invoices, all received

The in-transit SGA consignment — servo set, planetary gearboxes, PLC, SCADA and HMI — has arrived and is serial-tagged on its final tax invoice. Nothing remains in transit.

04 · Twelve-month roadmap

From CAD to a working service hub

The grant runs across a twelve-month schedule. Hardware procurement (Phase 2) is already complete — ahead of plan — so effort now shifts to mechanical assembly and control integration.

You are here · ~M3
05 · Impact targets

What success looks like

The pilot serves weavers in the Khordha district — Nuapatna and Balianta clusters — with a model designed to scale across Odisha's handloom belt and, eventually, pan-India.

70%
Faster pre-weaving thread preparation vs manual resist-tying
70%
Less yarn wastage via machine vision at 120 m/min
0.1mm
Motif placement accuracy so parallel threads align on the loom
70L
Clean water saved per kg of dyed thread — waterless pigment
53,472
Weavers in Odisha the model is designed to reach
30–50
Weavers served at the first pilot Service Hub in year one
1
Fully functional 10-ft automated coloring bed (BOT model)
L1–L6
NSQF Techno-Artisan training levels for local youth & artisans
06 · Engineering in progress

The work bed, digitised

The fabric-handling table has been finalised from the approved hand sketch into a dimensioned fabrication drawing. Print-head selection and design R&D continue in parallel.

Work Bed · fabric-handling table — 10 ft × 2 ft × 3.5 ft · roller-and-bearing sets at points A & B · issued for fabrication.
2 sets
Fabric rollers · A & B
1 ft
Roller drum dia · 34-in shaft
3 ft
Storage cabinet width
Ball brg.
Both ends, each roller

Print-head finalisation Ongoing

Selecting the dye-deposition head that matches Ikat pattern requirements before integrating it with the PLC-driven motion system.

  • Pattern resolution for handloom tie & dye motifs
  • Dye viscosity & flow compatibility
  • Throughput matched to roller tension
  • Sync with the AS132P-A PLC high-speed PTO

Design R&D Active

  • Motion & tension control — wrinkle-free transport
  • Vision registration & defect QC (Arduino MCUs)
  • Control logic & operator safety (SCADA/HMI)
  • IR drying & fixation zone sizing
